1.route_strategy: Example LLM route analysis: the aspirin target is disconnected at the aryl acetate bond, reducing the plan to salicylic-acid-derived material plus an acetyl donor. Route 1 uses acetic anhydride; this is chemically plausible, but the export alone does not validate conditions, impurity profile, or scale-up behavior.
key_disconnections: salicylic acid phenol acylation in the forward direction
reaction_sequence: Confirm salicylic acid or the displayed salicylate precursor identity and availability.
Run the O-acylation with a literature-backed acetyl donor and monitor conversion by LCMS/HPLC.
Quench, isolate, and verify aspirin identity, residual salicylic acid, and hydrolysis level.
1.conditions_strategy: screen acetic anhydride loading, weak base or acid catalysis, and aqueous quench timing.
1.yield_outlook: A moderate-to-high step yield may be plausible for a simple phenolic acylation, but this example reports it as a planning estimate rather than an experimental fact.
route_risks: hydrolysis of aspirin during workup or storage
incomplete O-acylation leaving salicylic acid impurity
acid chloride or anhydride handling, heat release, and corrosive byproducts
recommended_next_steps: search exact salicylic acid O-acylation precedent in SciFinder/Reaxys/internal ELN
check vendor, purity, and regulatory constraints for terminal precursors
run a small condition screen with HPLC assay before scale-up
1.chemist_verdict: go with verification: short and credible route, but conditions and analytical controls must be confirmed.

CC(=O)Oc1ccccc1C(=O)O.description: Example LLM molecule brief: this is a target in the displayed aspirin route set. The stock status is not a terminal precursor; confirm identity, purity, and route-specific functional-group compatibility before execution.

CC(=O)OC(C)=O.description: Example LLM molecule brief: this is a stock precursor in the displayed aspirin route set. The stock status is in stock; confirm identity, purity, and route-specific functional-group compatibility before execution.

[CH3:1][C:2](=[O:3])[O:4][c:5]1[cH:6][cH.description: Example LLM reaction analysis: the retrosynthetic step cleaves aspirin into a salicylate phenol component and an acetyl donor. In the forward direction, the phenolic oxygen is acylated to form the aryl acetate; the carboxylic acid must remain compatible with the selected conditions.
[CH3:1][C:2](=[O:3])[O:4][c:5]1[cH:6][cH.mechanistic_rationale: A nucleophilic phenolic oxygen attacks an activated acetyl electrophile, followed by collapse of the tetrahedral intermediate or equivalent acyl-transfer step.

suggested_conditions.reagents: acetic anhydride or acetyl chloride, selected by precedent
suggested_conditions.solvent: ethyl acetate, dichloromethane, or solvent-free/acid-catalyzed screen depending on scale and safety
suggested_conditions.base_or_catalyst: pyridine, triethylamine, DMAP, or acid catalysis as literature-supported options
suggested_conditions.temperature: 0-25 C addition control, then ambient to mild heating if needed
suggested_conditions.atmosphere: dry air or nitrogen depending on acetyl donor moisture sensitivity
suggested_conditions.workup: controlled aqueous quench, acid/base wash as appropriate, HPLC purity check

[CH3:1][C:2](=[O:3])[O:4][c:5]1[cH:6][cH.expected_yield_range: planning estimate only: roughly 50-90% for precedent-rich simple O-acylations, subject to substrate and process details
[CH3:1][C:2](=[O:3])[O:4][c:5]1[cH:6][cH.yield_rationale: Aspirin-like O-acylations are well precedented, but route export does not include experimental conditions, impurity data, or scale information.
selectivity_risks: competitive reaction or salt formation at the carboxylic acid
over-activation or side products if strongly activating reagents are used
safety_notes: acetyl chloride and acetic anhydride are corrosive and can release heat during quench
assess exotherm, ventilation, and acid-gas handling before scale-up
validation_plan: verify exact literature or internal precedent for salicylic acid acetylation
define impurity controls for salicylic acid and hydrolysis products
